title: systemd fails to start in centos:7 image
body:
/kind bug

**Description**
I can run systemd inside the `centos:8` container, but I cannot run it within the `centos:7` container.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Install podman on Fedora (I'm using Fedora 31)
2. Start systemd inside the CentOS 7 image:
```
podman run -it --privileged centos:7 /usr/sbin/init
```

**Describe the results you received:**
```
Failed to mount cgroup at /sys/fs/cgroup/systemd: Operation not permitted
[!!!!!!] Failed to mount API filesystems, freezing.
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
systemd starts in `centos:7`, the same as it does in `centos:8`

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**
This happens because I'm trying to test an Ansible role with molecule, and I need to test this Ansible code on both CentOS 7 and CentOS 8.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:            1.9.1
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.13.10
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
```

comments.body:
This is a known issue, though I don't know if it made it to the troubleshooting guide.

Basically, the systemd in the Centos 7 image is rather old (dates back ~6 years to the release of Cent 7, though it's seen backports since) - too old to support Cgroups v2. Fedora 31 uses Cgroups v2 by default, and in systemd containers, Podman forwards a subset of the host's cgroups into the container (so systemd within the container can set resource limits, among other things). Combine these together, and we're giving the old systemd too-new cgroups from the host, causing it to fail to start.

A few potential remediations: You can use a more recent Centos image (Centos 8 should have support for Cgroups v2). Alternatively, you could turn off Cgroups v2 on the host (which does have some disadvantages - no more resource limits for rootless containers).

We've looked into resolving this on the Podman side (there are some potential ways to mimic v1 cgroups within a container, even on a v2 host), but they're prohibitively complicated to implement.

Hope this helps!

Thank you for explaining this. It would be great to add this information to the troubleshooting guide.

Should I expect this to work on a Cgroups v1 host (like CentOS 7)?
Yes, I'd expect it to work fine on a Cent 7 host.
Thanks for the detailed answers. I'm closing this ticket now.

@ktdreyer It is covered in the troublshooting guide.
dependabot/go_modules/github.com/rootless-containers/rootlesskit-0.9.5

Is there a web URL where this is documented? I Googled rootlesskit like a noob but I cannot find this information in that project's [README](https://github.com/rootless-containers/rootlesskit).

Before I opened this ticket, when I was searching Google about this problem, I found docs like [rootless.md](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/rootless.md), but it was not clear to me if anything there fit my problem description.
I've updated that document with some language that might have helped me discover this: https://github.com/containers/libpod/pull/6195
